texto: For me, just being how old I am, I know I don't want to be a single mom. I really would rather make it a two-person job. But I've also come to terms with not being a mother at all. I'm actually really good with either direction that my life can take as being a valid experience.

texto: If you eat a lot of starchy foods, introduce a vegetable once a week, then twice a week, and then three times a week. Slowly fill your diet with new flavors. By the time you're ready to let go of whatever it is you want to let go of, you've got a full menu.

texto: Decades ago, women suffered through horrifying back-alley abortions. Or, they used dangerous methods when they had no other recourse. So when the Republican Party launched an all-out assault on women's health, pushing bills to limit access to vital services, we had to ask: Why is the GOP trying to send women back... to the back alley?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lisa Edelstein que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Decades ago, women suffered through horrifying back-alley abortions. Or, they used dangerous meth…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, women, party, com foco em termino e party.

texto: By learning about my body and making small, subtle changes, I find out what I enjoy and what is effective. I'm always finessing: adjusting my diet and my workouts. You have to figure out which exercises are fun and interesting and stimulate your brain - or else you'll never keep at them.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Lisa Edelstein sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “By learning about my body and making small, subtle changes, I find out what I enjoy and what is e…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, learning, changes, útil em momentos de mudanca e academia.
